_comment: Created with OWL2VOWL (version 0.3.7), http://vowl.visualdataweb.org [Additional Information added by WebVOWL Exporter Version: 1.1.7]

comments.undefined: This ontology reuses and integrates the SKOS (Simple Knowledge Organization System) vocabulary for the organisation of domain-specific concepts, and builds upon the CIDOC Conceptual Reference Model (CRM) for the representation of cultural heritage entities and events.

comment.undefined: The written files of the interviews
The geological or natural formation of the site or landscape.

definition.value: An ordered collection of concepts, where both the grouping and the ordering are meaningful.

scopeNote.value: Ordered collections can be used where you would like a set of concepts to be displayed in a specific order, and optionally under a 'node label'.

comment.undefined: A landscape that has been shaped or symbolically interpreted through cultural practices, beliefs, or artistic representation.

This class comprises pieces of land or sea floor. In contrast to the purely geometric notion of E53 Place, this class describes constellations of matter on the surface of the Earth or other celestial body, which can be represented by photographs, paintings and maps. Instances of E27 Site are composed of relatively immobile material items and features in a particular configuration at a particular location.
A location of outstanding geological, ecological, or scenic value, often protected for its environmental significance.
The area where traces of human occupation have been found.
A geographical unit that can aggregate multiple districts
The tip of an arrow.
A space open to visitors.
A narrow opening or crack typically resulting from geological processes.
Sounds of blowing wind
A natural underground space typically formed by the weathering of rock.

definition.value: A meaningful collection of concepts.

scopeNote.value: Labelled collections can be used where you would like a set of concepts to be displayed under a 'node label' in the hierarchy.

comment.undefined: A large body of water surrounded by land.
A specific location in space
A geological period dating from approximately 2.5 to 1.6 billion years ago.
A prototype or tool created to showcase a concept or functionality
Landscape characterised by open fields, low population and green areas with diverse vegetation.
Set of sounds typical from an environment.
A specific geographical area where large stone monuments or constructions, like stones circles or standing stones, are located.
Sounds of nature in its characteristic environment.
The restoration of components of the archaeological site.

definition.value: A set of concepts, optionally including statements about semantic relationships between those concepts.

example.value: Thesauri, classification schemes, subject heading lists, taxonomies, 'folksonomies', and other types of controlled vocabulary are all examples of concept schemes. Concept schemes are also embedded in glossaries and terminologies.

scopeNote.value: A concept scheme may be defined to include concepts from different sources.

comment.undefined: An arrangement of standing stones (menhirs) placed by humans.
3D model files
A ceramic drinking or storage vessel typical of Late Neolithic/Bronze Age.
Artefacts found in an archaeological site.
Video files
Audio files
A standing stone placed in the ground by humans.
A monument that preserves traces of human occupation
Sounds of tree branches moving.
Image FIles
A subtype of Beaker, typically larger and used in funerary contexts.
A specific and transitional period between prehistory and protohistory
The identification of the archaeological site.
Landscape includes physical and natural elements of an environment, as well as human elements such as built structures.
A place of importance recognised for its historical and cultural value
A landscape containing features made by humans. It can be protected as a cultural heritage site.

definition.value: An idea or notion; a unit of thought.

comment.undefined: A public entity whose efforts involve the acquisition, preservation, research and exhibition of objects with different types of values.
Object made with the purpose of fulfilling a religious / ritualistic / symbolic goal.
Object used in textile production.
A minor administrative division, sub part of Municipalities
A general-purpose manually shaped stone implement
A landscape predominantly shaped by natural processes, characterised by the absence of significant human intervention or built structures.
A software application designed to guide or assist users, often in cultural or educational contexts
A naturally raised area of land, smaller than a mountain.
An artistic and intellectual movement emphasizing nature, emotion, and individualism, often associated with the 19th century.
Conversations capturing personal or collective testimonies.
The later phase of the Neolithic period, associated with complex societies and megalithic monuments.
The sponsor of the archaeological intervention
Sounds of birds chirping.
Neolithic ceramic tradition characterized by incised decoration
The construction of the archaeological site.
An individual whose creative work reflects or engages with the heritage site
A subdivision of a region that can encompass multiple municipalities within boundaries
A specific period from pre-history marked by early farming and stone tools.
Roll ups of the exhibition
Tool made by knapping stone such as flint, quartz or mylonite
A professional whose expertise involves both on-site and off-site study of past human societies.
An archaeological monument built with standing megalithic stones
The earliest phase of the Neolithic, characterised by the initial adoption of agriculture, domestication of plants and animals, and the use of polished stone tools.
A story/saying that transmits meaning, sentiments and significant memories of a specific place
Committed participant without a professional background, actively involved in fieldwork
A feature regarding weather conditions.
A space, like a city, that contains physical and visual elements built by humans.
Technical examination of an archaeological site which involves the digging and removal of layers of earth and their associated artefacts.
Carved marks or decorations on menhirs.
The middle phase of the Bronze Age
A designated area preserved for its natural environment and public enjoyment.
An organised body responsible for research, preservation, or management of cultural/natural heritage.
Second-level administrative division after district,with its own jurisdiction and local government
The human usage or inhabitation of a site over a period of time.
A location where ancient religious or ceremonial/ritualistic practices were/are conducted.

An idol in the shape of a plaque (engraved stone
plaques)
Other sounds.

definition.value: Links a concept to a concept related by meaning.

scopeNote.value: This property should not be used directly, but as a super-property for all properties denoting a relationship of meaning between concepts.

comment.en: Narrower concepts are typically rendered as children in a concept hierarchy (tree).

definition.value: Relates a concept to a concept that is more specific in meaning.

scopeNote.value: By convention, skos:broader is only used to assert an immediate (i.e. direct) hierarchical link between two conceptual resources.

definition.value: Relates a concept to a concept with which there is an associative semantic relationship.
Relates a resource (for example a concept) to a concept scheme in which it is included.

scopeNote.value: A concept may be a member of more than one concept scheme.

definition.value: Relates, by convention, a concept scheme to a concept which is topmost in the broader/narrower concept hierarchies for that scheme, providing an entry point to these hierarchies.

comment.en: skos:exactMatch is disjoint with each of the properties skos:broadMatch and skos:relatedMatch.

definition.value: skos:exactMatch is used to link two concepts, indicating a high degree of confidence that the concepts can be used interchangeably across a wide range of information retrieval applications. skos:exactMatch is a transitive property, and is a sub-property of skos:closeMatch.
skos:broadMatch is used to state a hierarchical mapping link between two conceptual resources in different concept schemes.
A notation, also known as classification code, is a string of characters such as "T58.5" or "303.4833" used to uniquely identify a concept within the scope of a given concept scheme.

scopeNote.value: By convention, skos:notation is used with a typed literal in the object position of the triple.

definition.value: skos:relatedMatch is used to state an associative mapping link between two conceptual resources in different concept schemes.
Relates a concept to the concept scheme that it is a top level concept of.
skos:narrowMatch is used to state a hierarchical mapping link between two conceptual resources in different concept schemes.

comment.en:
For any resource, every item in the list given as the value of the
      skos:memberList property is also a value of the skos:member property.

definition.value: Relates an ordered collection to the RDF list containing its members.
Relates a collection to one of its members.

skos:closeMatch is used to link two concepts that are sufficiently similar that they can be used interchangeably in some information retrieval applications. In order to avoid the possibility of "compound errors" when combining mappings across more than two concept schemes, skos:closeMatch is not declared to be a transitive property.
skos:broaderTransitive is a transitive superproperty of skos:broader.

scopeNote.value: By convention, skos:broaderTransitive is not used to make assertions. Rather, the properties can be used to draw inferences about the transitive closure of the hierarchical relation, which is useful e.g. when implementing a simple query expansion algorithm in a search application.

comment.en: Broader concepts are typically rendered as parents in a concept hierarchy (tree).

definition.value: Relates a concept to a concept that is more general in meaning.

scopeNote.value: By convention, skos:broader is only used to assert an immediate (i.e. direct) hierarchical link between two conceptual resources.

comment.en: These concept mapping relations mirror semantic relations, and the data model defined below is similar (with the exception of skos:exactMatch) to the data model defined for semantic relations. A distinct vocabulary is provided for concept mapping relations, to provide a convenient way to differentiate links within a concept scheme from links between concept schemes. However, this pattern of usage is not a formal requirement of the SKOS data model, and relies on informal definitions of best practice.

definition.value: Relates two concepts coming, by convention, from different schemes, and that have comparable meanings
skos:narrowerTransitive is a transitive superproperty of skos:narrower.

scopeNote.value: By convention, skos:narrowerTransitive is not used to make assertions. Rather, the properties can be used to draw inferences about the transitive closure of the hierarchical relation, which is useful e.g. when implementing a simple query expansion algorithm in a search application.
